title: Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2017 (EESM17)
description: The Ear-EEG Sleep Monitoring 2017 (EESM17) dataset comprises nightly polysomnographic recordings from 9 healthy participants, combining conventional EEG, EOG, and chin EMG with 14 ear-EEG electrodes. The dataset includes both wake-state recordings (subjects relaxing before sleep) and sleep-state recordings (overnight monitoring at participants' homes), with sleep scoring initiated at lights out. This resource enables investigation of ear-based EEG for sleep monitoring applications.
methods_description: Partial polysomnography measurements were collected during nightly recordings at participants' homes. Data acquisition included conventional EEG, EOG, and chin EMG combined with 14 ear-EEG electrodes. Recordings consisted of two sessions per subject: a wake task (relaxation period before sleep) and a sleep task (overnight monitoring beginning at lights out). Equipment was mounted in the afternoon prior to recordings.
